151:    // Registers to hold state etc.
152:    reg                        [31:0] address_counter;     // Holds address for next Wishbone access
153:    reg                         [5:0] bit_count;            // How many bits have been shifted in/out

157:    reg [`DBG_AXI_REGSELECT_SIZE-1:0] internal_register_select;  // Holds index of currently selected register

161:    // Control signals for the various counters / registers / state machines
162:    reg                               addr_sel;                 // Selects data for address_counter. 0 = data_register_i, 1 = incremented address count
163:    reg                               addr_ct_en;               // Enable signal for address counter register
164:    reg                               op_reg_en;                // Enable signal for 'operation' register
165:    reg                               bit_ct_en;                // enable bit counter
166:    reg                               bit_ct_rst;               // reset (zero) bit count register
167:    reg                               word_ct_sel;              // Selects data for byte counter.  0 = data_register_i, 1 = decremented byte count
168:    reg                               word_ct_en;               // Enable byte counter register
169:    reg                               out_reg_ld_en;            // Enable parallel load of data_out_shift_reg
170:    reg                               out_reg_shift_en;         // Enable shift of data_out_shift_reg
171:    reg                               out_reg_data_sel;         // 0 = BIU data, 1 = internal register data
172:    reg                        [1:0]  tdo_output_sel;           // Selects signal to send to TDO.  0 = ready bit, 1 = output register, 2 = CRC match, 3 = CRC shift reg.
173:    reg                               biu_strobe;               // Indicates that the bus unit should latch data and start a transaction
174:    reg                               crc_clr;                  // resets CRC module
175:    reg                               crc_en;                   // does 1-bit iteration in CRC module
176:    reg                               crc_in_sel;               // selects incoming write data (=0) or outgoing read data (=1)as input to CRC module
177:    reg                               crc_shift_en;             // CRC reg is also it's own output shift register; this enables a shift
178:    reg                               regsel_ld_en;             // Reg. select register load enable
179:    reg                               intreg_ld_en;             // load enable for internal registers
180:    reg                               error_reg_en;             // Tells the error register to check for and latch a bus error
181:    reg                               biu_clr_err;              // Allows FSM to reset BIU, to clear the biu_err bit which may have been set on the last transaction of the last burst.
182: 
183:    // Status signals
184:    wire                               word_count_zero;         // true when byte counter is zero
185:    wire                               bit_count_max;           // true when bit counter is equal to current word size
186:    wire                               module_cmd;              // inverse of MSB of data_register_i. 1 means current cmd not for top level (but is for us)
187:    wire                               biu_ready;               // indicates that the BIU has finished the last command
188:    wire                               biu_err;                 // indicates wishbone error during BIU transaction
189:    wire                               burst_read;              // True when the input_data_i reg has a valid burst read  instruction for this module 
190:    wire                               burst_write;             // True when the input_data_i reg has a valid burst write instruction for this module
191:    wire                               intreg_instruction;      // True when the input_data_i reg has a valid internal register instruction
192:    wire                               intreg_write;            // True when the input_data_i reg has an internal register write op

194:    wire                               crc_match;               // indicates whether data_register_i matches computed CRC
195:    wire                               bit_count_32;            // true when bit count register == 32, for CRC after burst writes
196: 
197:    // Intermediate signals

203:    wire                        [15:0] decremented_word_count;

207:    wire                        [63:0] data_to_biu;             // from data_register_i
208:    wire                        [63:0]               data_from_biu;           // to data_out_shift_register
209:    wire                        [31:0] crc_data_out;            // output of CRC module, to output shift register
210:    wire                               crc_data_in;             // input to CRC module, either data_register_i[52] or data_out_shift_reg[0]

212:    wire [`DBG_AXI_REGSELECT_SIZE-1:0] reg_select_data;         // from data_register_i, input to internal register select register

85:    // Breakpoint is latched and synchronized. Stall is set and latched.
86:    // This is done in the CPU clock domain, because the JTAG clock (TCK) is
87:    // irregular.  By only allowing bp_i to set (but not reset) the stall_bp
88:    // signal, we insure that the CPU will remain in the stalled state until
89:    // the debug host can read the state.
90:     always @ (posedge cpu_clk_i or negedge cpu_rstn_i)

96:             for (int i=0;i<NB_CORES;i++)

98:                 if(bp_i[i])

100:                 else if(stall_reg_cpu[i])

103:         end
104:     end

137:    // bp_i forces a stall immediately on a breakpoint
138:    // stall_bp holds the stall until the debug host acts
139:    // stall_reg_cpu allows the debug host to control a stall.
140:    assign cpu_stall_o = bp_i | stall_bp | stall_reg_cpu;

86:    // This is done in the CPU clock domain, because the JTAG clock (TCK) is

88:    // signal, we insure that the CPU will remain in the stalled state until
89:    // the debug host can read the state.
90:     always @ (posedge cpu_clk_i or negedge cpu_rstn_i)
91:     begin
92:         if(~cpu_rstn_i)
93:             stall_bp <= '0;
94:         else
95:         begin
96:             for (int i=0;i<NB_CORES;i++)
97:             begin
98:                 if(bp_i[i])
99:                     stall_bp[i] <= 1'b1;
100:                 else if(stall_reg_cpu[i])
101:                     stall_bp[i] <= 1'b0;
102:             end
103:         end
104:     end
105: 
106: 
107:    // Synchronizing
108:    always @ (posedge tck_i or negedge trstn_i)
109:      begin
110:     if (~trstn_i)
111:       begin
112:          stall_bp_csff <= '0;
113:          stall_bp_tck  <= '0;
114:       end
115:     else
116:       begin
117:          stall_bp_csff <= stall_bp;
118:          stall_bp_tck  <= stall_bp_csff;
119:       end
120:      end
121: 
122: 
123:    always @ (posedge cpu_clk_i or negedge cpu_rstn_i)
124:      begin
125:     if (~cpu_rstn_i)
126:       begin
127:          stall_reg_csff <= '0;
128:          stall_reg_cpu  <= '0;
129:       end
130:     else
131:       begin
132:          stall_reg_csff <= stall_reg;
133:          stall_reg_cpu  <= stall_reg_csff;
134:       end
135:      end
136: 
137:    // bp_i forces a stall immediately on a breakpoint
138:    // stall_bp holds the stall until the debug host acts
139:    // stall_reg_cpu allows the debug host to control a stall.
140:    assign cpu_stall_o = bp_i | stall_bp | stall_reg_cpu;

105:    reg [15:0]      word_count;          // bytes remaining in current burst command

107:    reg [31:0]      data_out_shift_reg;  // parallel-load output shift register

116:    reg                       bit_ct_en;         // enable bit counter

123:    reg [1:0]                   tdo_output_sel;  // Selects signal to send to TDO.  0 = ready bit, 1 = output register, 2 = CRC match, 3 = CRC shift reg.
124:    reg                       biu_strobe;      // Indicates that the bus unit should latch data and start a transaction
125:    reg                       crc_clr;         // resets CRC module
126:    reg                       crc_en;          // does 1-bit iteration in CRC module
127:    reg                       crc_in_sel;      // selects incoming write data (=0) or outgoing read data (=1)as input to CRC module
128:    reg                       crc_shift_en;    // CRC reg is also it's own output shift register; this enables a shift
129:    reg                       regsel_ld_en;    // Reg. select register load enable
130:    reg                       intreg_ld_en;    // load enable for internal registers
131:    reg                       cpusel_ld_en;

134:    // Status signals
135:    wire                   word_count_zero;   // true when byte counter is zero

137:    wire                   module_cmd;        // inverse of MSB of data_register_i. 1 means current cmd not for top level (but is for us)
138:    wire                   biu_ready;         // indicates that the BIU has finished the last command
139:    wire                   burst_instruction; // True when the input_data_i reg has a valid burst instruction for this module
140:    wire                   intreg_instruction; // True when the input_data_i reg has a valid internal register instruction
141:    wire                   intreg_write;       // True when the input_data_i reg has an internal register write op
142:    wire                   rd_op;              // True when operation in the opcode reg is a read, false when a write
143:    wire                   crc_match;         // indicates whether data_register_i matches computed CRC
144:    wire                   bit_count_32;      // true when bit count register == 32, for CRC after burst writes
145: 
146:    // Intermediate signals
147:    wire [5:0]                   word_size_bits;         // 8,16, or 32.  Decoded from 'operation'
148:    wire [2:0]                   address_increment;      // How much to add to the address counter each iteration
149:    wire [32:0]                   incremented_address;   // value of address counter plus 'word_size'
150:    wire [31:0]                   data_to_addr_counter;  // output of the mux in front of the address counter inputs

513:            else if(bit_count_32) module_next_state <= STATE_Wmatch;
514:            else module_next_state <= STATE_Wcrc;

516:

68: module adbg_or1k_status_reg  #( 
69:         parameter NB_CORES = 4
70:     ) (
71:     input                       tck_i,
72:     input                       trstn_i,
73:     input                       we_i,
74:     input                       cpu_clk_i,
75:     input                       cpu_rstn_i,
76:     input  logic         [15:0] data_i,
77:     input  logic [NB_CORES-1:0] bp_i,
78:     output logic [NB_CORES-1:0] ctrl_reg_o,
79:     output logic [NB_CORES-1:0] cpu_stall_o
80: );
81: 
82:    reg   [NB_CORES-1:0] stall_bp, stall_bp_csff, stall_bp_tck;
83:    reg   [NB_CORES-1:0] stall_reg, stall_reg_csff, stall_reg_cpu;
84: 
85:    // Breakpoint is latched and synchronized. Stall is set and latched.
86:    // This is done in the CPU clock domain, because the JTAG clock (TCK) is
87:    // irregular.  By only allowing bp_i to set (but not reset) the stall_bp
88:    // signal, we insure that the CPU will remain in the stalled state until
89:    // the debug host can read the state.
90:     always @ (posedge cpu_clk_i or negedge cpu_rstn_i)
91:     begin
92:         if(~cpu_rstn_i)
93:             stall_bp <= '0;
94:         else
95:         begin
96:             for (int i=0;i<NB_CORES;i++)
97:             begin
98:                 if(bp_i[i])
99:                     stall_bp[i] <= 1'b1;
100:                 else if(stall_reg_cpu[i])
101:                     stall_bp[i] <= 1'b0;
102:             end
103:         end
104:     end
105: 
106: 
107:    // Synchronizing
108:    always @ (posedge tck_i or negedge trstn_i)
109:      begin
110:     if (~trstn_i)
111:       begin
112:          stall_bp_csff <= '0;
113:          stall_bp_tck  <= '0;
114:       end
115:     else

487:            else if(module_select_i && data_register_i[56]) module_next_state <= STATE_Wburst; // Got a start bit

68: module adbg_or1k_status_reg  #( 
69:         parameter NB_CORES = 4
70:     ) (
71:     input                       tck_i,
72:     input                       trstn_i,
73:     input                       we_i,
74:     input                       cpu_clk_i,
75:     input                       cpu_rstn_i,
76:     input  logic         [15:0] data_i,
77:     input  logic [NB_CORES-1:0] bp_i,
78:     output logic [NB_CORES-1:0] ctrl_reg_o,
79:     output logic [NB_CORES-1:0] cpu_stall_o
80: );
81: 
82:    reg   [NB_CORES-1:0] stall_bp, stall_bp_csff, stall_bp_tck;
83:    reg   [NB_CORES-1:0] stall_reg, stall_reg_csff, stall_reg_cpu;
84: 
85:    // Breakpoint is latched and synchronized. Stall is set and latched.
86:    // This is done in the CPU clock domain, because the JTAG clock (TCK) is
87:    // irregular.  By only allowing bp_i to set (but not reset) the stall_bp
88:    // signal, we insure that the CPU will remain in the stalled state until
89:    // the debug host can read the state.
90:     always @ (posedge cpu_clk_i or negedge cpu_rstn_i)
91:     begin
92:         if(~cpu_rstn_i)
93:             stall_bp <= '0;
94:         else
95:         begin
96:             for (int i=0;i<NB_CORES;i++)
97:             begin
98:                 if(bp_i[i])
99:                     stall_bp[i] <= 1'b1;
100:                 else if(stall_reg_cpu[i])
101:                     stall_bp[i] <= 1'b0;
102:             end
103:         end
104:     end
105: 
106: 
107:    // Synchronizing
108:    always @ (posedge tck_i or negedge trstn_i)
109:      begin
110:     if (~trstn_i)
111:       begin
112:          stall_bp_csff <= '0;
113:          stall_bp_tck  <= '0;
114:       end
115:     else
116:       begin
117:          stall_bp_csff <= stall_bp;
118:          stall_bp_tck  <= stall_bp_csff;
119:       end
120:      end
121: 
122: 
123:    always @ (posedge cpu_clk_i or negedge cpu_rstn_i)
124:      begin
125:     if (~cpu_rstn_i)
126:       begin
127:          stall_reg_csff <= '0;
128:          stall_reg_cpu  <= '0;
129:       end
130:     else
131:       begin
132:          stall_reg_csff <= stall_reg;
133:          stall_reg_cpu  <= stall_reg_csff;
134:       end
135:      end
136: 
137:    // bp_i forces a stall immediately on a breakpoint
138:    // stall_bp holds the stall until the debug host acts
139:    // stall_reg_cpu allows the debug host to control a stall.
140:    assign cpu_stall_o = bp_i | stall_bp | stall_reg_cpu;

432: 
433:    // Determination of next state; purely combinatorial
434:    always @ (module_state or module_select_i or update_dr_i or capture_dr_i or shift_dr_i or operation_in[2]
435:          or word_count_zero or bit_count_max or data_register_i[56] or bit_count_32 or biu_ready
436:          or module_cmd or intreg_write or decremented_word_count or burst_instruction)

438:     case(module_state)
439:       STATE_idle:

529:    // Outputs of state machine, pure combinatorial
530:    always @ (module_state or module_next_state or module_select_i or update_dr_i or capture_dr_i or shift_dr_i or operation_in[2]
531:          or word_count_zero or bit_count_max or data_register_i[52] or biu_ready or intreg_instruction or burst_instruction
532:          or module_cmd or intreg_write or decremented_word_count)
533:      begin
534:     // Default everything to 0, keeps the case statement simple
535:     addr_sel <= 1'b1;  // Selects data for address_counter. 0 = data_register_i, 1 = incremented address count
536:     addr_ct_en <= 1'b0;  // Enable signal for address counter register
537:     op_reg_en <= 1'b0;  // Enable signal for 'operation' register
538:     bit_ct_en <= 1'b0;  // enable bit counter
539:     bit_ct_rst <= 1'b0;  // reset (zero) bit count register
540:     word_ct_sel <= 1'b1;  // Selects data for byte counter.  0 = data_register_i, 1 = decremented byte count
541:     word_ct_en <= 1'b0;   // Enable byte counter register
542:     out_reg_ld_en <= 1'b0;  // Enable parallel load of data_out_shift_reg
543:     out_reg_shift_en <= 1'b0;  // Enable shift of data_out_shift_reg
544:     tdo_output_sel <= 2'b1;   // 1 = data reg, 0 = biu_ready, 2 = crc_match, 3 = CRC data
545:     biu_strobe <= 1'b0;
546:     crc_clr <= 1'b0;
547:     crc_en <= 1'b0;      // add the input bit to the CRC calculation
548:     crc_in_sel <= 1'b0;  // 0 = tdo, 1 = tdi
549:     crc_shift_en <= 1'b0;
550:     out_reg_data_sel <= 1'b1;  // 0 = BIU data, 1 = internal register data
551:     regsel_ld_en <= 1'b0;
552:     cpusel_ld_en <= 1'b0;
553:     intreg_ld_en <= 1'b0;
554:     top_inhibit_o <= 1'b0;  // Don't disable the top-level module in the default case

556:     case(module_state)
557:       STATE_idle:
558:         begin
559:            addr_sel <= 1'b0;
560:            word_ct_sel <= 1'b0;
561: 
562:            // Operations for internal registers - stay in idle state
563:            if(module_select_i & shift_dr_i) out_reg_shift_en <= 1'b1; // For module regs
564:            if(module_select_i & capture_dr_i)
565:            begin
566:              out_reg_data_sel <= 1'b1;  // select internal register data
567:              out_reg_ld_en <= 1'b1;   // For module regs
568:            end
569:            if(module_select_i & module_cmd & update_dr_i) begin
570:              if(intreg_instruction) regsel_ld_en <= 1'b1;  // For module regs
571:              if(intreg_write)       intreg_ld_en <= 1'b1;  // For module regs
572:              if(burst_instruction)  cpusel_ld_en <= 1'b1;
573:            end
574: 
575:            // Burst operations
576:            if(module_next_state != STATE_idle) begin  // Do the same to receive read or write opcode
577:              addr_ct_en <= 1'b1;
578:              op_reg_en <= 1'b1;
579:              bit_ct_rst <= 1'b1;
580:              word_ct_en <= 1'b1;

68: module adbg_or1k_status_reg  #( 
69:         parameter NB_CORES = 4
70:     ) (
71:     input                       tck_i,
72:     input                       trstn_i,
73:     input                       we_i,
74:     input                       cpu_clk_i,
75:     input                       cpu_rstn_i,
76:     input  logic         [15:0] data_i,
77:     input  logic [NB_CORES-1:0] bp_i,
78:     output logic [NB_CORES-1:0] ctrl_reg_o,
79:     output logic [NB_CORES-1:0] cpu_stall_o
80: );
81: 
82:    reg   [NB_CORES-1:0] stall_bp, stall_bp_csff, stall_bp_tck;
83:    reg   [NB_CORES-1:0] stall_reg, stall_reg_csff, stall_reg_cpu;
84: 
85:    // Breakpoint is latched and synchronized. Stall is set and latched.
86:    // This is done in the CPU clock domain, because the JTAG clock (TCK) is
87:    // irregular.  By only allowing bp_i to set (but not reset) the stall_bp
88:    // signal, we insure that the CPU will remain in the stalled state until
89:    // the debug host can read the state.
90:     always @ (posedge cpu_clk_i or negedge cpu_rstn_i)
91:     begin
92:         if(~cpu_rstn_i)
93:             stall_bp <= '0;
94:         else
95:         begin
96:             for (int i=0;i<NB_CORES;i++)
97:             begin
98:                 if(bp_i[i])
99:                     stall_bp[i] <= 1'b1;
100:                 else if(stall_reg_cpu[i])
101:                     stall_bp[i] <= 1'b0;
102:             end
103:         end
104:     end
105: 
106: 
107:    // Synchronizing
108:    always @ (posedge tck_i or negedge trstn_i)
109:      begin
110:     if (~trstn_i)
111:       begin
112:          stall_bp_csff <= '0;
113:          stall_bp_tck  <= '0;
114:       end
115:     else
116:       begin
117:          stall_bp_csff <= stall_bp;
118:          stall_bp_tck  <= stall_bp_csff;
119:       end
120:      end
121: 
122: 
123:    always @ (posedge cpu_clk_i or negedge cpu_rstn_i)
124:      begin
125:     if (~cpu_rstn_i)
126:       begin
127:          stall_reg_csff <= '0;
128:          stall_reg_cpu  <= '0;
129:       end
130:     else
131:       begin
132:          stall_reg_csff <= stall_reg;
133:          stall_reg_cpu  <= stall_reg_csff;
134:       end
135:      end
136: 
137:    // bp_i forces a stall immediately on a breakpoint
138:    // stall_bp holds the stall until the debug host acts
139:    // stall_reg_cpu allows the debug host to control a stall.
140:    assign cpu_stall_o = bp_i | stall_bp | stall_reg_cpu;
141:

271:         .axi_master_b_user(axi_master_b_user),

68: // Module interface
69: module adbg_or1k_module #(
70: 		parameter NB_CORES = 4
71:     ) (
72:     // JTAG signals
73:     input  logic                       tck_i,
74:     output logic                       module_tdo_o,
75:     input  logic                       tdi_i,  // This is only used by the CRC module - data_register_i[MSB] is delayed a cycle
76: 
77:     // TAP states
78:     input  logic                       capture_dr_i,
79:     input  logic                       shift_dr_i,
80:     input  logic                       update_dr_i,
81: 
82:     input  logic                [56:0] data_register_i,
83:     input  logic                       module_select_i,
84:     output logic                       top_inhibit_o,
85:     input  logic                       trstn_i,
86: 
87:     // Interface to OR1200 debug unit
88:     input                              cpu_clk_i,    // 'bus' style interface to SPRs
89:     input                              cpu_rstn_i,
90:     output logic [NB_CORES-1:0] [15:0] cpu_addr_o,
91:     input  logic [NB_CORES-1:0] [31:0] cpu_data_i,
92:     output logic [NB_CORES-1:0] [31:0] cpu_data_o,
93:     input  logic [NB_CORES-1:0]        cpu_bp_i,
94:     output logic [NB_CORES-1:0]        cpu_stall_o,
95:     output logic [NB_CORES-1:0]        cpu_stb_o,
96:     output logic [NB_CORES-1:0]        cpu_we_o,
97:     input  logic [NB_CORES-1:0]        cpu_ack_i
98: );

101: 
102:    // Registers to hold state etc.
103:    reg [31:0]      address_counter;     // Holds address for next Wishbone access
104:    reg [5:0]      bit_count;            // How many bits have been shifted in/out
105:    reg [15:0]      word_count;          // bytes remaining in current burst command
106:    reg [3:0]      operation;            // holds the current command (rd/wr, word size)
107:    reg [31:0]      data_out_shift_reg;  // parallel-load output shift register
108:    reg  [2:0] internal_register_select;  // Holds index of currently selected register
109:    wire [NB_CORES-1:0]                   internal_reg_status;  // Holds CPU stall and reset status - signal is output of separate module
110: 
111: 
112:    // Control signals for the various counters / registers / state machines
113:    reg                       addr_sel;          // Selects data for address_counter. 0 = data_register_i, 1 = incremented address count
114:    reg                       addr_ct_en;        // Enable signal for address counter register
115:    reg                       op_reg_en;         // Enable signal for 'operation' register
116:    reg                       bit_ct_en;         // enable bit counter
117:    reg                       bit_ct_rst;        // reset (zero) bit count register
118:    reg                       word_ct_sel;       // Selects data for byte counter.  0 = data_register_i, 1 = decremented byte count
119:    reg                       word_ct_en;        // Enable byte counter register
120:    reg                       out_reg_ld_en;     // Enable parallel load of data_out_shift_reg
121:    reg                       out_reg_shift_en;  // Enable shift of data_out_shift_reg
122:    reg                       out_reg_data_sel;  // 0 = BIU data, 1 = internal register data
123:    reg [1:0]                   tdo_output_sel;  // Selects signal to send to TDO.  0 = ready bit, 1 = output register, 2 = CRC match, 3 = CRC shift reg.
124:    reg                       biu_strobe;      // Indicates that the bus unit should latch data and start a transaction
125:    reg                       crc_clr;         // resets CRC module
126:    reg                       crc_en;          // does 1-bit iteration in CRC module
127:    reg                       crc_in_sel;      // selects incoming write data (=0) or outgoing read data (=1)as input to CRC module
128:    reg                       crc_shift_en;    // CRC reg is also it's own output shift register; this enables a shift
129:    reg                       regsel_ld_en;    // Reg. select register load enable
130:    reg                       intreg_ld_en;    // load enable for internal registers
131:    reg                       cpusel_ld_en;
132: 
133: 
134:    // Status signals
135:    wire                   word_count_zero;   // true when byte counter is zero
136:    wire                   bit_count_max;     // true when bit counter is equal to current word size
137:    wire                   module_cmd;        // inverse of MSB of data_register_i. 1 means current cmd not for top level (but is for us)
138:    wire                   biu_ready;         // indicates that the BIU has finished the last command
139:    wire                   burst_instruction; // True when the input_data_i reg has a valid burst instruction for this module
140:    wire                   intreg_instruction; // True when the input_data_i reg has a valid internal register instruction
141:    wire                   intreg_write;       // True when the input_data_i reg has an internal register write op
142:    wire                   rd_op;              // True when operation in the opcode reg is a read, false when a write
143:    wire                   crc_match;         // indicates whether data_register_i matches computed CRC
144:    wire                   bit_count_32;      // true when bit count register == 32, for CRC after burst writes
145: 
146:    // Intermediate signals
147:    wire [5:0]                   word_size_bits;         // 8,16, or 32.  Decoded from 'operation'
148:    wire [2:0]                   address_increment;      // How much to add to the address counter each iteration
149:    wire [32:0]                   incremented_address;   // value of address counter plus 'word_size'
150:    wire [31:0]                   data_to_addr_counter;  // output of the mux in front of the address counter inputs
151:    wire [15:0]                   data_to_word_counter;  // output of the mux in front of the byte counter input
152:    wire [15:0]                   decremented_word_count;
153:    wire [31:0]                   address_data_in;       // from data_register_i
154:    wire [15:0]                   count_data_in;         // from data_register_i
155:    wire [3:0]                   operation_in;          // from data_register_i
156:    wire [31:0]                   data_to_biu;           // from data_register_i
157:    wire [31:0]                   data_from_biu;         // to data_out_shift_register
158:    wire [31:0]                   crc_data_out;          // output of CRC module, to output shift register
159:    wire                   crc_data_in;                  // input to CRC module, either data_register_i[52] or data_out_shift_reg[0]
160:    wire                   crc_serial_out;
161:    wire [2:0] reg_select_data; // from data_register_i, input to internal register select register
162:    wire [31:0]                    out_reg_data;           // parallel input to the output shift register
163:    reg [31:0]                    data_from_internal_reg;  // data from internal reg. MUX to output shift register
164:    wire                    status_reg_wr;
165: 
166: 
167:     logic          [3:0] cpu_select;
168:     logic          [3:0] cpu_select_in;
169: 
170:     logic [15:0] status_reg_data;
171: 
172:    // Definition of machine state values.
173:    // Don't worry too much about the state encoding, the synthesis tool
174:    // will probably re-encode it anyway.
175:    enum logic [3:0] {STATE_idle,STATE_Rbegin,STATE_Rready,STATE_Rstatus,STATE_Rburst,STATE_Wready,STATE_Wwait,STATE_Wburst,STATE_Wstatus,STATE_Rcrc,STATE_Wcrc,STATE_Wmatch} module_state,module_next_state;
176: 
177:    /////////////////////////////////////////////////
178:    // Combinatorial assignments
179: 
180:     assign module_cmd      =  ~(data_register_i[56]);
181:     assign operation_in    = data_register_i[55:52];
182:     assign cpu_select_in   = data_register_i[51:48];
183:     assign address_data_in = data_register_i[47:16];
184:     assign count_data_in   = data_register_i[15:0];
185: 
186:     assign data_to_biu     = {tdi_i,data_register_i[56:26]};
187: 
188:     assign reg_select_data = data_register_i[51:49];
189:     assign status_reg_data = data_register_i[48:33];
190: 
191:    ////////////////////////////////////////////////
192:           // Operation decoder
193: 
194:     // These are only used before the operation is latched, so decode them from operation_in
195:     assign burst_instruction  = (operation_in == `DBG_OR1K_CMD_BWRITE32) | (operation_in == `DBG_OR1K_CMD_BREAD32);
196:     assign intreg_instruction = (operation_in == `DBG_OR1K_CMD_IREG_WR)  | (operation_in == `DBG_OR1K_CMD_IREG_SEL);
197:     assign intreg_write       = (operation_in == `DBG_OR1K_CMD_IREG_WR);
198: 
199:    // These are constant, the CPU module only does 32-bit accesses
200:    assign     word_size_bits = 5'd31;  // Bits is actually bits-1, to make the FSM easier
201:    assign     address_increment = 3'd1;  // This is only used to increment the address.  SPRs are word-addressed.
202: 
203:    // This is the only thing that actually needs to be saved and 'decoded' from the latched opcode
204:    // It goes to the BIU each time a transaction is started.
205:    assign     rd_op = operation[2];
206: 
207:

209:    // Module-internal register select register (no, that's not redundant.)
210:    // Also internal register output MUX
211: 
212:     always @ (posedge tck_i or negedge trstn_i)
213:     begin
214:         if(~trstn_i)
215:             internal_register_select = 'h0;
216:         else if(regsel_ld_en)
217:             internal_register_select = reg_select_data;
218:     end
219: 
220:    ////////////////////////////////////////////////
221:    // CPU select register
222:    //
223: 
224:     always @ (posedge tck_i or negedge trstn_i)
225:     begin
226:         if(~trstn_i)
227:             cpu_select = 'h0;
228:         else if(cpusel_ld_en)
229:             cpu_select = cpu_select_in;
230:     end
231: 
232:     // This is completely unnecessary here, since the module has only 1 internal
233:     // register.  However, to make the module expandable, it is included anyway.
234:     always @ (internal_register_select or internal_reg_status)
235:     begin
236:         case(internal_register_select)
237:             `DBG_OR1K_INTREG_STATUS:
238:                 data_from_internal_reg = {{(32-NB_CORES){1'b0}}, internal_reg_status};
239:           default:

247:    // Module-internal registers
248:    // These have generic read/write/select code, but
249:    // individual registers may have special behavior, defined here.
250: 
251:    // This is the status register, which holds the reset and stall states.
252: 
253:    assign status_reg_wr = (intreg_ld_en & (reg_select_data == `DBG_OR1K_INTREG_STATUS));
254: 
255:    adbg_or1k_status_reg #(
256: 			.NB_CORES(NB_CORES)
257: 			) or1k_statusreg_i (
258:                      .data_i(status_reg_data),
259:                      .we_i(status_reg_wr),
260:                      .tck_i(tck_i),
261:                      .bp_i(cpu_bp_i),
262:                      .trstn_i(trstn_i),
263:                      .cpu_clk_i(cpu_clk_i),
264:                      .cpu_rstn_i(cpu_rstn_i),

268: 
269: 
270:     ///////////////////////////////////////////////
271:     // Address counter
272:     ///////////////////////////////////////////////
273: 
274:     assign data_to_addr_counter = (addr_sel) ? incremented_address[31:0] : address_data_in;
275:     assign incremented_address  = address_counter + address_increment;
276: 
277:    // Technically, since this data (sometimes) comes from the input shift reg, we should latch on
278:    // negedge, per the JTAG spec. But that makes things difficult when incrementing.
279:     always @ (posedge tck_i or negedge trstn_i)  // JTAG spec specifies latch on negative edge in UPDATE_DR state
280:     begin
281:         if(~trstn_i)
282:             address_counter <= 32'h0;
283:         else if(addr_ct_en)
284:             address_counter <= data_to_addr_counter;

397: 
398:     /////////////////////////////////////
399:     // CRC module
400:     /////////////////////////////////////
401: 
402:      assign crc_data_in = (crc_in_sel) ? tdi_i : data_out_shift_reg[0];  // MUX, write or read data
403: 
404:     adbg_crc32 or1k_crc_i
405:     (
406:         .clk(tck_i),
407:         .data(crc_data_in),
408:         .enable(crc_en),
409:         .shift(crc_shift_en),
410:         .clr(crc_clr),
411:         .rstn(trstn_i),
412:         .crc_out(crc_data_out),
413:         .serial_out(crc_serial_out)

421:    ////////////////////////////////////////
422: 
423:    // sequential part of the FSM
424:    always @ (posedge tck_i or negedge trstn_i)
425:      begin
426:     if(~trstn_i)
427:       module_state <= STATE_idle;
428:     else
429:       module_state <= module_next_state;
430:      end
431: 
432: 
433:    // Determination of next state; purely combinatorial
434:    always @ (module_state or module_select_i or update_dr_i or capture_dr_i or shift_dr_i or operation_in[2]
435:          or word_count_zero or bit_count_max or data_register_i[56] or bit_count_32 or biu_ready
436:          or module_cmd or intreg_write or decremented_word_count or burst_instruction)
437:      begin
438:     case(module_state)
439:       STATE_idle:
440:         begin
441:            if(module_cmd && module_select_i && update_dr_i && burst_instruction && operation_in[2])
442:                 module_next_state <= STATE_Rbegin;
443:            else if(module_cmd && module_select_i && update_dr_i && burst_instruction)
444:                 module_next_state <= STATE_Wready;
445:            else
446:                 module_next_state <= STATE_idle;
447:         end
448: 
449:       STATE_Rbegin:
450:         begin
451:            if(word_count_zero) module_next_state <= STATE_idle;  // set up a burst of size 0, illegal.
452:            else module_next_state <= STATE_Rready;
453:         end
454:       STATE_Rready:
455:         begin
456:            if(module_select_i && capture_dr_i) module_next_state <= STATE_Rstatus;
457:            else module_next_state <= STATE_Rready;
458:         end
459:       STATE_Rstatus:
460:         begin
461:            if(update_dr_i) module_next_state <= STATE_idle;
462:            else if (biu_ready) module_next_state <= STATE_Rburst;
463:            else module_next_state <= STATE_Rstatus;
464:         end
465:       STATE_Rburst:
466:         begin
467:            if(update_dr_i) module_next_state <= STATE_idle;
468:            else if(bit_count_max && word_count_zero) module_next_state <= STATE_Rcrc;
469:            else module_next_state <= STATE_Rburst;
470:         end
471:       STATE_Rcrc:
472:         begin
473:            if(update_dr_i) module_next_state <= STATE_idle;
474:            // This doubles as the 'recovery' state, so stay here until update_dr_i.
475:            else module_next_state <= STATE_Rcrc;
476:         end
477: 
478:       STATE_Wready:
479:         begin
480:            if(word_count_zero) module_next_state <= STATE_idle;
481:            else if(module_select_i && capture_dr_i) module_next_state <= STATE_Wwait;
482:            else module_next_state <= STATE_Wready;
483:         end
484:       STATE_Wwait:
485:         begin
486:            if(update_dr_i)  module_next_state <= STATE_idle;  // client terminated early
487:            else if(module_select_i && data_register_i[56]) module_next_state <= STATE_Wburst; // Got a start bit
488:            else module_next_state <= STATE_Wwait;
489:         end
490:       STATE_Wburst:
491:         begin
492:            if(update_dr_i)  module_next_state <= STATE_idle;  // client terminated early
493:            else if(bit_count_max)
494:              begin
495:                 if(word_count_zero) module_next_state <= STATE_Wcrc;
496:                 else module_next_state <= STATE_Wburst;
497:              end
498:            else module_next_state <= STATE_Wburst;
499:         end
500:       STATE_Wstatus:
501:         begin
502:            if(update_dr_i)  module_next_state <= STATE_idle;  // client terminated early
503:            else if(word_count_zero) module_next_state <= STATE_Wcrc;
504:            // can't wait until bus ready if multiple devices in chain...
505:            // Would have to read postfix_bits, then send another start bit and push it through

507:            else module_next_state <= STATE_Wburst;
508:         end
509: 
510:       STATE_Wcrc:
511:         begin
512:            if(update_dr_i)  module_next_state <= STATE_idle;  // client terminated early
513:            else if(bit_count_32) module_next_state <= STATE_Wmatch;
514:            else module_next_state <= STATE_Wcrc;
515:         end
516: 
517:       STATE_Wmatch:
518:         begin
519:            if(update_dr_i)  module_next_state <= STATE_idle;
520:            // This doubles as our recovery state, stay here until update_dr_i
521:            else module_next_state <= STATE_Wmatch;
522:         end

66: `include "adbg_or1k_defines.v"

68: module adbg_or1k_status_reg  #( 
69:         parameter NB_CORES = 4
70:     ) (
71:     input                       tck_i,
72:     input                       trstn_i,
73:     input                       we_i,
74:     input                       cpu_clk_i,
75:     input                       cpu_rstn_i,
76:     input  logic         [15:0] data_i,
77:     input  logic [NB_CORES-1:0] bp_i,
78:     output logic [NB_CORES-1:0] ctrl_reg_o,
79:     output logic [NB_CORES-1:0] cpu_stall_o
80: );
81: 
82:    reg   [NB_CORES-1:0] stall_bp, stall_bp_csff, stall_bp_tck;
83:    reg   [NB_CORES-1:0] stall_reg, stall_reg_csff, stall_reg_cpu;
84: 
85:    // Breakpoint is latched and synchronized. Stall is set and latched.
86:    // This is done in the CPU clock domain, because the JTAG clock (TCK) is
87:    // irregular.  By only allowing bp_i to set (but not reset) the stall_bp
88:    // signal, we insure that the CPU will remain in the stalled state until
89:    // the debug host can read the state.
90:     always @ (posedge cpu_clk_i or negedge cpu_rstn_i)
91:     begin
92:         if(~cpu_rstn_i)
93:             stall_bp <= '0;
94:         else
95:         begin
96:             for (int i=0;i<NB_CORES;i++)
97:             begin
98:                 if(bp_i[i])
99:                     stall_bp[i] <= 1'b1;
100:                 else if(stall_reg_cpu[i])
101:                     stall_bp[i] <= 1'b0;
102:             end
103:         end
104:     end
105: 
106: 
107:    // Synchronizing
108:    always @ (posedge tck_i or negedge trstn_i)
109:      begin
110:     if (~trstn_i)
111:       begin
112:          stall_bp_csff <= '0;
113:          stall_bp_tck  <= '0;
114:       end
115:     else
116:       begin
117:          stall_bp_csff <= stall_bp;
118:          stall_bp_tck  <= stall_bp_csff;
119:       end
120:      end
121: 
122: 
123:    always @ (posedge cpu_clk_i or negedge cpu_rstn_i)
124:      begin
125:     if (~cpu_rstn_i)
126:       begin
127:          stall_reg_csff <= '0;
128:          stall_reg_cpu  <= '0;
129:       end
130:     else
131:       begin
132:          stall_reg_csff <= stall_reg;
133:          stall_reg_cpu  <= stall_reg_csff;
134:       end
135:      end
136: 
137:    // bp_i forces a stall immediately on a breakpoint
138:    // stall_bp holds the stall until the debug host acts
139:    // stall_reg_cpu allows the debug host to control a stall.
140:    assign cpu_stall_o = bp_i | stall_bp | stall_reg_cpu;

52:         // JTAG signals
53:         input  logic           tck_i,
54:         input  logic           tdi_i,
55:         output logic           tdo_o,
56:         input  logic           trstn_i,
57: 
58:         // TAP states

62:         input  logic                   capture_dr_i,

102:    // Registers to hold state etc.
103:    reg [31:0]      address_counter;     // Holds address for next Wishbone access
104:    reg [5:0]      bit_count;            // How many bits have been shifted in/out
105:    reg [15:0]      word_count;          // bytes remaining in current burst command
106:    reg [3:0]      operation;            // holds the current command (rd/wr, word size)
107:    reg [31:0]      data_out_shift_reg;  // parallel-load output shift register
108:    reg  [2:0] internal_register_select;  // Holds index of currently selected register
109:    wire [NB_CORES-1:0]                   internal_reg_status;  // Holds CPU stall and reset status - signal is output of separate module
110: 
111: 
112:    // Control signals for the various counters / registers / state machines

114:    reg                       addr_ct_en;        // Enable signal for address counter register

116:    reg                       bit_ct_en;         // enable bit counter
117:    reg                       bit_ct_rst;        // reset (zero) bit count register

119:    reg                       word_ct_en;        // Enable byte counter register
120:    reg                       out_reg_ld_en;     // Enable parallel load of data_out_shift_reg
121:    reg                       out_reg_shift_en;  // Enable shift of data_out_shift_reg
122:    reg                       out_reg_data_sel;  // 0 = BIU data, 1 = internal register data
123:    reg [1:0]                   tdo_output_sel;  // Selects signal to send to TDO.  0 = ready bit, 1 = output register, 2 = CRC match, 3 = CRC shift reg.
124:    reg                       biu_strobe;      // Indicates that the bus unit should latch data and start a transaction
125:    reg                       crc_clr;         // resets CRC module
126:    reg                       crc_en;          // does 1-bit iteration in CRC module
127:    reg                       crc_in_sel;      // selects incoming write data (=0) or outgoing read data (=1)as input to CRC module
128:    reg                       crc_shift_en;    // CRC reg is also it's own output shift register; this enables a shift
129:    reg                       regsel_ld_en;    // Reg. select register load enable
130:    reg                       intreg_ld_en;    // load enable for internal registers
131:    reg                       cpusel_ld_en;
132: 
133: 
134:    // Status signals
135:    wire                   word_count_zero;   // true when byte counter is zero
136:    wire                   bit_count_max;     // true when bit counter is equal to current word size
137:    wire                   module_cmd;        // inverse of MSB of data_register_i. 1 means current cmd not for top level (but is for us)
138:    wire                   biu_ready;         // indicates that the BIU has finished the last command
139:    wire                   burst_instruction; // True when the input_data_i reg has a valid burst instruction for this module
140:    wire                   intreg_instruction; // True when the input_data_i reg has a valid internal register instruction
141:    wire                   intreg_write;       // True when the input_data_i reg has an internal register write op
142:    wire                   rd_op;              // True when operation in the opcode reg is a read, false when a write
143:    wire                   crc_match;         // indicates whether data_register_i matches computed CRC
144:    wire                   bit_count_32;      // true when bit count register == 32, for CRC after burst writes
145: 
146:    // Intermediate signals
147:    wire [5:0]                   word_size_bits;         // 8,16, or 32.  Decoded from 'operation'
148:    wire [2:0]                   address_increment;      // How much to add to the address counter each iteration
149:    wire [32:0]                   incremented_address;   // value of address counter plus 'word_size'
150:    wire [31:0]                   data_to_addr_counter;  // output of the mux in front of the address counter inputs
151:    wire [15:0]                   data_to_word_counter;  // output of the mux in front of the byte counter input
152:    wire [15:0]                   decremented_word_count;
153:    wire [31:0]                   address_data_in;       // from data_register_i
154:    wire [15:0]                   count_data_in;         // from data_register_i
155:    wire [3:0]                   operation_in;          // from data_register_i
156:    wire [31:0]                   data_to_biu;           // from data_register_i
157:    wire [31:0]                   data_from_biu;         // to data_out_shift_register
158:    wire [31:0]                   crc_data_out;          // output of CRC module, to output shift register
159:    wire                   crc_data_in;                  // input to CRC module, either data_register_i[52] or data_out_shift_reg[0]
160:    wire                   crc_serial_out;
161:    wire [2:0] reg_select_data; // from data_register_i, input to internal register select register
162:    wire [31:0]                    out_reg_data;           // parallel input to the output shift register
163:    reg [31:0]                    data_from_internal_reg;  // data from internal reg. MUX to output shift register
164:    wire                    status_reg_wr;

172:    // Definition of machine state values.

175:    enum logic [3:0] {STATE_idle,STATE_Rbegin,STATE_Rready,STATE_Rstatus,STATE_Rburst,STATE_Wready,STATE_Wwait,STATE_Wburst,STATE_Wstatus,STATE_Rcrc,STATE_Wcrc,STATE_Wmatch} module_state,module_next_state;

182:     assign cpu_select_in   = data_register_i[51:48];
183:     assign address_data_in = data_register_i[47:16];
184:     assign count_data_in   = data_register_i[15:0];
185: 
186:     assign data_to_biu     = {tdi_i,data_register_i[56:26]};

191:    ////////////////////////////////////////////////

193: 
194:     // These are only used before the operation is latched, so decode them from operation_in
195:     assign burst_instruction  = (operation_in == `DBG_OR1K_CMD_BWRITE32) | (operation_in == `DBG_OR1K_CMD_BREAD32);
196:     assign intreg_instruction = (operation_in == `DBG_OR1K_CMD_IREG_WR)  | (operation_in == `DBG_OR1K_CMD_IREG_SEL);
197:     assign intreg_write       = (operation_in == `DBG_OR1K_CMD_IREG_WR);
198: 
199:    // These are constant, the CPU module only does 32-bit accesses
200:    assign     word_size_bits = 5'd31;  // Bits is actually bits-1, to make the FSM easier
201:    assign     address_increment = 3'd1;  // This is only used to increment the address.  SPRs are word-addressed.
202: 
203:    // This is the only thing that actually needs to be saved and 'decoded' from the latched opcode
204:    // It goes to the BIU each time a transaction is started.
205:    assign     rd_op = operation[2];

208:    ////////////////////////////////////////////////
209:    // Module-internal register select register (no, that's not redundant.)
210:    // Also internal register output MUX
211: 
212:     always @ (posedge tck_i or negedge trstn_i)

232:     // This is completely unnecessary here, since the module has only 1 internal
233:     // register.  However, to make the module expandable, it is included anyway.
234:     always @ (internal_register_select or internal_reg_status)
235:     begin
236:         case(internal_register_select)
237:             `DBG_OR1K_INTREG_STATUS:
238:                 data_from_internal_reg = {{(32-NB_CORES){1'b0}}, internal_reg_status};
239:           default:
240:                 data_from_internal_reg = {{(32-NB_CORES){1'b0}}, internal_reg_status};
241:         endcase
242:     end

249:    // individual registers may have special behavior, defined here.

251:    // This is the status register, which holds the reset and stall states.
252: 
253:    assign status_reg_wr = (intreg_ld_en & (reg_select_data == `DBG_OR1K_INTREG_STATUS));

487:            else if(module_select_i && data_register_i[56]) module_next_state <= STATE_Wburst; // Got a start bit
488:            else module_next_state <= STATE_Wwait;

490:       STATE_Wburst:

512:            if(update_dr_i)  module_next_state <= STATE_idle;  // client terminated early
513:            else if(bit_count_32) module_next_state <= STATE_Wmatch;
514:            else module_next_state <= STATE_Wcrc;
515:         end
516: 
517:       STATE_Wmatch:

519:            if(update_dr_i)  module_next_state <= STATE_idle;
520:            // This doubles as our recovery state, stay here until update_dr_i

66: `include "adbg_or1k_defines.v"

68: module adbg_or1k_status_reg  #( 
69:         parameter NB_CORES = 4
70:     ) (
71:     input                       tck_i,
72:     input                       trstn_i,
73:     input                       we_i,
74:     input                       cpu_clk_i,
75:     input                       cpu_rstn_i,
76:     input  logic         [15:0] data_i,
77:     input  logic [NB_CORES-1:0] bp_i,
78:     output logic [NB_CORES-1:0] ctrl_reg_o,
79:     output logic [NB_CORES-1:0] cpu_stall_o
80: );
81: 
82:    reg   [NB_CORES-1:0] stall_bp, stall_bp_csff, stall_bp_tck;
83:    reg   [NB_CORES-1:0] stall_reg, stall_reg_csff, stall_reg_cpu;
84: 
85:    // Breakpoint is latched and synchronized. Stall is set and latched.
86:    // This is done in the CPU clock domain, because the JTAG clock (TCK) is
87:    // irregular.  By only allowing bp_i to set (but not reset) the stall_bp
88:    // signal, we insure that the CPU will remain in the stalled state until
89:    // the debug host can read the state.
90:     always @ (posedge cpu_clk_i or negedge cpu_rstn_i)
91:     begin
92:         if(~cpu_rstn_i)
93:             stall_bp <= '0;
94:         else
95:         begin
96:             for (int i=0;i<NB_CORES;i++)
97:             begin
98:                 if(bp_i[i])
99:                     stall_bp[i] <= 1'b1;
100:                 else if(stall_reg_cpu[i])
101:                     stall_bp[i] <= 1'b0;
102:             end
103:         end
104:     end
105: 
106: 
107:    // Synchronizing
108:    always @ (posedge tck_i or negedge trstn_i)
109:      begin
110:     if (~trstn_i)
111:       begin
112:          stall_bp_csff <= '0;
113:          stall_bp_tck  <= '0;
114:       end
115:     else
116:       begin
117:          stall_bp_csff <= stall_bp;
118:          stall_bp_tck  <= stall_bp_csff;
119:       end
120:      end
121: 
122: 
123:    always @ (posedge cpu_clk_i or negedge cpu_rstn_i)
124:      begin
125:     if (~cpu_rstn_i)
126:       begin
127:          stall_reg_csff <= '0;
128:          stall_reg_cpu  <= '0;
129:       end
130:     else
131:       begin
132:          stall_reg_csff <= stall_reg;
133:          stall_reg_cpu  <= stall_reg_csff;
134:       end
135:      end
136: 
137:    // bp_i forces a stall immediately on a breakpoint
138:    // stall_bp holds the stall until the debug host acts
139:    // stall_reg_cpu allows the debug host to control a stall.
140:    assign cpu_stall_o = bp_i | stall_bp | stall_reg_cpu;
141:

134: 		output logic                        axi_master_b_ready
135:     );
136: 
137: 
138:    wire                tdo_axi;
139:    wire                tdo_cpu;
140: 
141:    // Registers
142:    reg [`DBG_TOP_MODULE_DATA_LEN-1:0] input_shift_reg;  // 1 bit sel/cmd, 4 bit opcode, 32 bit address, 16 bit length = 53 bits
143:    reg                          [4:0] module_id_reg;    // Module selection register
144: 
145: 
146:    // Control signals
147:    wire               select_cmd;      // True when the command (registered at Update_DR) is for top level/module selection
148:    wire         [4:0] module_id_in;    // The part of the input_shift_register to be used as the module select data

122:    reg                       out_reg_data_sel;  // 0 = BIU data, 1 = internal register data
123:    reg [1:0]                   tdo_output_sel;  // Selects signal to send to TDO.  0 = ready bit, 1 = output register, 2 = CRC match, 3 = CRC shift reg.
124:    reg                       biu_strobe;      // Indicates that the bus unit should latch data and start a transaction

421:    ////////////////////////////////////////
422: 
423:    // sequential part of the FSM
424:    always @ (posedge tck_i or negedge trstn_i)
425:      begin
426:     if(~trstn_i)
427:       module_state <= STATE_idle;
428:     else
429:       module_state <= module_next_state;
430:      end
431: 
432: 
433:    // Determination of next state; purely combinatorial
434:    always @ (module_state or module_select_i or update_dr_i or capture_dr_i or shift_dr_i or operation_in[2]
435:          or word_count_zero or bit_count_max or data_register_i[56] or bit_count_32 or biu_ready
436:          or module_cmd or intreg_write or decremented_word_count or burst_instruction)
437:      begin
438:     case(module_state)
439:       STATE_idle:
440:         begin
441:            if(module_cmd && module_select_i && update_dr_i && burst_instruction && operation_in[2])
442:                 module_next_state <= STATE_Rbegin;
443:            else if(module_cmd && module_select_i && update_dr_i && burst_instruction)
444:                 module_next_state <= STATE_Wready;
445:            else
446:                 module_next_state <= STATE_idle;
447:         end
448: 
449:       STATE_Rbegin:
450:         begin
451:            if(word_count_zero) module_next_state <= STATE_idle;  // set up a burst of size 0, illegal.
452:            else module_next_state <= STATE_Rready;
453:         end
454:       STATE_Rready:
455:         begin
456:            if(module_select_i && capture_dr_i) module_next_state <= STATE_Rstatus;
457:            else module_next_state <= STATE_Rready;
458:         end
459:       STATE_Rstatus:
460:         begin
461:            if(update_dr_i) module_next_state <= STATE_idle;
462:            else if (biu_ready) module_next_state <= STATE_Rburst;
463:            else module_next_state <= STATE_Rstatus;
464:         end
465:       STATE_Rburst:
466:         begin
467:            if(update_dr_i) module_next_state <= STATE_idle;
468:            else if(bit_count_max && word_count_zero) module_next_state <= STATE_Rcrc;
469:            else module_next_state <= STATE_Rburst;
470:         end
471:       STATE_Rcrc:
472:         begin
473:            if(update_dr_i) module_next_state <= STATE_idle;
474:            // This doubles as the 'recovery' state, so stay here until update_dr_i.
475:            else module_next_state <= STATE_Rcrc;
476:         end
477: 
478:       STATE_Wready:
479:         begin
480:            if(word_count_zero) module_next_state <= STATE_idle;
481:            else if(module_select_i && capture_dr_i) module_next_state <= STATE_Wwait;
482:            else module_next_state <= STATE_Wready;
483:         end
484:       STATE_Wwait:
485:         begin
486:            if(update_dr_i)  module_next_state <= STATE_idle;  // client terminated early
487:            else if(module_select_i && data_register_i[56]) module_next_state <= STATE_Wburst; // Got a start bit
488:            else module_next_state <= STATE_Wwait;
489:         end
490:       STATE_Wburst:
491:         begin
492:            if(update_dr_i)  module_next_state <= STATE_idle;  // client terminated early
493:            else if(bit_count_max)
494:              begin
495:                 if(word_count_zero) module_next_state <= STATE_Wcrc;
496:                 else module_next_state <= STATE_Wburst;
497:              end
498:            else module_next_state <= STATE_Wburst;
499:         end
500:       STATE_Wstatus:
501:         begin
502:            if(update_dr_i)  module_next_state <= STATE_idle;  // client terminated early
503:            else if(word_count_zero) module_next_state <= STATE_Wcrc;
504:            // can't wait until bus ready if multiple devices in chain...
505:            // Would have to read postfix_bits, then send another start bit and push it through

507:            else module_next_state <= STATE_Wburst;
508:         end
509: 
510:       STATE_Wcrc:
511:         begin
512:            if(update_dr_i)  module_next_state <= STATE_idle;  // client terminated early
513:            else if(bit_count_32) module_next_state <= STATE_Wmatch;
514:            else module_next_state <= STATE_Wcrc;
515:         end
516: 
517:       STATE_Wmatch:
518:         begin
519:            if(update_dr_i)  module_next_state <= STATE_idle;
520:            // This doubles as our recovery state, stay here until update_dr_i
521:            else module_next_state <= STATE_Wmatch;
522:         end

68: module adbg_or1k_status_reg  #( 
69:         parameter NB_CORES = 4
70:     ) (
71:     input                       tck_i,
72:     input                       trstn_i,
73:     input                       we_i,
74:     input                       cpu_clk_i,
75:     input                       cpu_rstn_i,
76:     input  logic         [15:0] data_i,
77:     input  logic [NB_CORES-1:0] bp_i,
78:     output logic [NB_CORES-1:0] ctrl_reg_o,
79:     output logic [NB_CORES-1:0] cpu_stall_o
80: );
81: 
82:    reg   [NB_CORES-1:0] stall_bp, stall_bp_csff, stall_bp_tck;
83:    reg   [NB_CORES-1:0] stall_reg, stall_reg_csff, stall_reg_cpu;
84: 
85:    // Breakpoint is latched and synchronized. Stall is set and latched.
86:    // This is done in the CPU clock domain, because the JTAG clock (TCK) is
87:    // irregular.  By only allowing bp_i to set (but not reset) the stall_bp
88:    // signal, we insure that the CPU will remain in the stalled state until
89:    // the debug host can read the state.
90:     always @ (posedge cpu_clk_i or negedge cpu_rstn_i)
91:     begin
92:         if(~cpu_rstn_i)
93:             stall_bp <= '0;
94:         else
95:         begin
96:             for (int i=0;i<NB_CORES;i++)
97:             begin
98:                 if(bp_i[i])
99:                     stall_bp[i] <= 1'b1;
100:                 else if(stall_reg_cpu[i])
101:                     stall_bp[i] <= 1'b0;
102:             end
103:         end
104:     end
105: 
106: 
107:    // Synchronizing
108:    always @ (posedge tck_i or negedge trstn_i)
109:      begin
110:     if (~trstn_i)
111:       begin
112:          stall_bp_csff <= '0;
113:          stall_bp_tck  <= '0;
114:       end
115:     else
116:       begin
117:          stall_bp_csff <= stall_bp;
118:          stall_bp_tck  <= stall_bp_csff;
119:       end
120:      end
121: 
122: 
123:    always @ (posedge cpu_clk_i or negedge cpu_rstn_i)
124:      begin
125:     if (~cpu_rstn_i)
126:       begin
127:          stall_reg_csff <= '0;
128:          stall_reg_cpu  <= '0;
129:       end
130:     else
131:       begin
132:          stall_reg_csff <= stall_reg;
133:          stall_reg_cpu  <= stall_reg_csff;
134:       end
135:      end
136: 
137:    // bp_i forces a stall immediately on a breakpoint
138:    // stall_bp holds the stall until the debug host acts
139:    // stall_reg_cpu allows the debug host to control a stall.
140:    assign cpu_stall_o = bp_i | stall_bp | stall_reg_cpu;

68: module adbg_or1k_status_reg  #( 
69:         parameter NB_CORES = 4
70:     ) (
71:     input                       tck_i,
72:     input                       trstn_i,
73:     input                       we_i,
74:     input                       cpu_clk_i,
75:     input                       cpu_rstn_i,
76:     input  logic         [15:0] data_i,
77:     input  logic [NB_CORES-1:0] bp_i,
78:     output logic [NB_CORES-1:0] ctrl_reg_o,
79:     output logic [NB_CORES-1:0] cpu_stall_o
80: );
81: 
82:    reg   [NB_CORES-1:0] stall_bp, stall_bp_csff, stall_bp_tck;
83:    reg   [NB_CORES-1:0] stall_reg, stall_reg_csff, stall_reg_cpu;
84: 
85:    // Breakpoint is latched and synchronized. Stall is set and latched.
86:    // This is done in the CPU clock domain, because the JTAG clock (TCK) is
87:    // irregular.  By only allowing bp_i to set (but not reset) the stall_bp
88:    // signal, we insure that the CPU will remain in the stalled state until
89:    // the debug host can read the state.
90:     always @ (posedge cpu_clk_i or negedge cpu_rstn_i)
91:     begin
92:         if(~cpu_rstn_i)
93:             stall_bp <= '0;
94:         else
95:         begin
96:             for (int i=0;i<NB_CORES;i++)
97:             begin
98:                 if(bp_i[i])
99:                     stall_bp[i] <= 1'b1;
100:                 else if(stall_reg_cpu[i])
101:                     stall_bp[i] <= 1'b0;
102:             end
103:         end
104:     end
105: 
106: 
107:    // Synchronizing
108:    always @ (posedge tck_i or negedge trstn_i)
109:      begin
110:     if (~trstn_i)
111:       begin
112:          stall_bp_csff <= '0;
113:          stall_bp_tck  <= '0;
114:       end
115:     else
116:       begin
117:          stall_bp_csff <= stall_bp;
118:          stall_bp_tck  <= stall_bp_csff;
119:       end
120:      end
121: 
122: 
123:    always @ (posedge cpu_clk_i or negedge cpu_rstn_i)
124:      begin
125:     if (~cpu_rstn_i)
126:       begin
127:          stall_reg_csff <= '0;
128:          stall_reg_cpu  <= '0;
129:       end
130:     else
131:       begin
132:          stall_reg_csff <= stall_reg;
133:          stall_reg_cpu  <= stall_reg_csff;
134:       end
135:      end
136: 
137:    // bp_i forces a stall immediately on a breakpoint
138:    // stall_bp holds the stall until the debug host acts
139:    // stall_reg_cpu allows the debug host to control a stall.
140:    assign cpu_stall_o = bp_i | stall_bp | stall_reg_cpu;

386: /**********************************************************************************
387: *                                                                                 *
388: *   jtag_ir:  JTAG Instruction Register                                           *
389: *                                                                                 *
390: **********************************************************************************/
391: reg [`IR_LENGTH-1:0]  jtag_ir;          // Instruction register
392: reg [`IR_LENGTH-1:0]  latched_jtag_ir; //, latched_jtag_ir_neg;
393: wire                  instruction_tdo;
394: 
395: always @ (posedge tck_pad_i or negedge trstn_pad_i)
396: begin
397:   if(trstn_pad_i == 0)
398:     jtag_ir[`IR_LENGTH-1:0] <= `IR_LENGTH'b0;
399:   else if (test_logic_reset == 1)
400: 	jtag_ir[`IR_LENGTH-1:0] <= `IR_LENGTH'b0;
401:   else if(capture_ir)
402:     jtag_ir <= 4'b0101;          // This value is fixed for easier fault detection
403:   else if(shift_ir)
404:     jtag_ir[`IR_LENGTH-1:0] <= {tdi_pad_i, jtag_ir[`IR_LENGTH-1:1]};
405: end
406: 
407: assign instruction_tdo = jtag_ir[0];  // This is latched on a negative TCK edge after the output MUX
408: 
409: // Updating jtag_ir (Instruction Register)
410: // jtag_ir should be latched on FALLING EDGE of TCK when capture_ir == 1
411: always @ (posedge s_clk_neg or negedge trstn_pad_i)
412: begin
413:   if(trstn_pad_i == 0)
414:     latched_jtag_ir <= `IDCODE;   // IDCODE selected after reset
415:   else if (test_logic_reset)
416:     latched_jtag_ir <= `IDCODE;   // IDCODE selected after reset
417:   else if(update_ir)
418:     latched_jtag_ir <= jtag_ir;
419: end
420: 
421: /**********************************************************************************
422: *                                                                                 *
423: *   End: jtag_ir                                                                  *
424: *                                                                                 *
425: **********************************************************************************/

440:     idcode_reg <= `IDCODE_VALUE;   // IDCODE selected after reset
441:   else if (test_logic_reset)
442:     idcode_reg <= `IDCODE_VALUE;   // IDCODE selected after reset
443:   else if(idcode_select & capture_dr)
444:     idcode_reg <=  `IDCODE_VALUE;
445:   else if(idcode_select & shift_dr)
446:     idcode_reg <=  {tdi_pad_i, idcode_reg[31:1]};
447: 
448: end

465: reg   bypass_reg;  // This is a 1-bit register
466: 
467: always @ (posedge tck_pad_i or negedge trstn_pad_i)

471:   else if (test_logic_reset == 1)

473:   else if (bypass_select & capture_dr)

475:   else if(bypass_select & shift_dr)

479: assign bypassed_tdo = bypass_reg;   // This is latched on a negative TCK edge after the output MUX

498:   mbist_select            = 1'b0;
499:   debug_select            = 1'b0;
500:   bypass_select           = 1'b0;
501: 
502:   case(latched_jtag_ir)    /* synthesis parallel_case */ 
503:     `EXTEST:            extest_select           = 1'b1;    // External test
504:     `SAMPLE_PRELOAD:    sample_preload_select   = 1'b1;    // Sample preload
505:     `IDCODE:            idcode_select           = 1'b1;    // ID Code
506:     `MBIST:             mbist_select            = 1'b1;    // Mbist test
507:     `DEBUG:             debug_select            = 1'b1;    // Debug
508:     `BYPASS:            bypass_select           = 1'b1;    // BYPASS
509:     default:            bypass_select           = 1'b1;    // BYPASS
510:   endcase

521: always @ (shift_ir or instruction_tdo or latched_jtag_ir or idcode_tdo or

531:         `DEBUG:             tdo_mux_out = debug_tdo_i;      // Debug
532:         `SAMPLE_PRELOAD:    tdo_mux_out = bs_chain_tdo_i;   // Sampling/Preloading
533:         `EXTEST:            tdo_mux_out = bs_chain_tdo_i;   // External test
534:         `MBIST:             tdo_mux_out = mbist_tdo_i;      // Mbist test
535:         default:            tdo_mux_out = bypassed_tdo;     // BYPASS instruction
536:       endcase
537:     end
538: end

542: always @ (posedge s_clk_neg or negedge trstn_pad_i)
543: begin
544:   if (trstn_pad_i == 0)
545:     tdo_pad_o <= 1'b0;

102:    // Registers to hold state etc.
103:    reg [31:0]      address_counter;     // Holds address for next Wishbone access
104:    reg [5:0]      bit_count;            // How many bits have been shifted in/out
105:    reg [15:0]      word_count;          // bytes remaining in current burst command
106:    reg [3:0]      operation;            // holds the current command (rd/wr, word size)
107:    reg [31:0]      data_out_shift_reg;  // parallel-load output shift register
108:    reg  [2:0] internal_register_select;  // Holds index of currently selected register
109:    wire [NB_CORES-1:0]                   internal_reg_status;  // Holds CPU stall and reset status - signal is output of separate module
110: 
111: 
112:    // Control signals for the various counters / registers / state machines
113:    reg                       addr_sel;          // Selects data for address_counter. 0 = data_register_i, 1 = incremented address count
114:    reg                       addr_ct_en;        // Enable signal for address counter register
115:    reg                       op_reg_en;         // Enable signal for 'operation' register
116:    reg                       bit_ct_en;         // enable bit counter
117:    reg                       bit_ct_rst;        // reset (zero) bit count register
118:    reg                       word_ct_sel;       // Selects data for byte counter.  0 = data_register_i, 1 = decremented byte count
119:    reg                       word_ct_en;        // Enable byte counter register
120:    reg                       out_reg_ld_en;     // Enable parallel load of data_out_shift_reg
121:    reg                       out_reg_shift_en;  // Enable shift of data_out_shift_reg
122:    reg                       out_reg_data_sel;  // 0 = BIU data, 1 = internal register data
123:    reg [1:0]                   tdo_output_sel;  // Selects signal to send to TDO.  0 = ready bit, 1 = output register, 2 = CRC match, 3 = CRC shift reg.
124:    reg                       biu_strobe;      // Indicates that the bus unit should latch data and start a transaction
125:    reg                       crc_clr;         // resets CRC module
126:    reg                       crc_en;          // does 1-bit iteration in CRC module
127:    reg                       crc_in_sel;      // selects incoming write data (=0) or outgoing read data (=1)as input to CRC module
128:    reg                       crc_shift_en;    // CRC reg is also it's own output shift register; this enables a shift
129:    reg                       regsel_ld_en;    // Reg. select register load enable
130:    reg                       intreg_ld_en;    // load enable for internal registers
131:    reg                       cpusel_ld_en;

135:    wire                   word_count_zero;   // true when byte counter is zero
136:    wire                   bit_count_max;     // true when bit counter is equal to current word size
137:    wire                   module_cmd;        // inverse of MSB of data_register_i. 1 means current cmd not for top level (but is for us)
138:    wire                   biu_ready;         // indicates that the BIU has finished the last command

141:    wire                   intreg_write;       // True when the input_data_i reg has an internal register write op
142:    wire                   rd_op;              // True when operation in the opcode reg is a read, false when a write

386: /**********************************************************************************
387: *                                                                                 *
388: *   jtag_ir:  JTAG Instruction Register                                           *
389: *                                                                                 *
390: **********************************************************************************/
391: reg [`IR_LENGTH-1:0]  jtag_ir;          // Instruction register
392: reg [`IR_LENGTH-1:0]  latched_jtag_ir; //, latched_jtag_ir_neg;
393: wire                  instruction_tdo;
394: 
395: always @ (posedge tck_pad_i or negedge trstn_pad_i)
396: begin
397:   if(trstn_pad_i == 0)
398:     jtag_ir[`IR_LENGTH-1:0] <= `IR_LENGTH'b0;
399:   else if (test_logic_reset == 1)
400: 	jtag_ir[`IR_LENGTH-1:0] <= `IR_LENGTH'b0;
401:   else if(capture_ir)
402:     jtag_ir <= 4'b0101;          // This value is fixed for easier fault detection

408: 
409: // Updating jtag_ir (Instruction Register)
410: // jtag_ir should be latched on FALLING EDGE of TCK when capture_ir == 1
411: always @ (posedge s_clk_neg or negedge trstn_pad_i)
412: begin
413:   if(trstn_pad_i == 0)
414:     latched_jtag_ir <= `IDCODE;   // IDCODE selected after reset
415:   else if (test_logic_reset)
416:     latched_jtag_ir <= `IDCODE;   // IDCODE selected after reset
417:   else if(update_ir)
418:     latched_jtag_ir <= jtag_ir;
419: end
420: 
421: /**********************************************************************************
422: *                                                                                 *
423: *   End: jtag_ir                                                                  *
424: *                                                                                 *
425: **********************************************************************************/

505:     `IDCODE:            idcode_select           = 1'b1;    // ID Code
506:     `MBIST:             mbist_select            = 1'b1;    // Mbist test
507:     `DEBUG:             debug_select            = 1'b1;    // Debug
508:     `BYPASS:            bypass_select           = 1'b1;    // BYPASS

532:         `SAMPLE_PRELOAD:    tdo_mux_out = bs_chain_tdo_i;   // Sampling/Preloading
533:         `EXTEST:            tdo_mux_out = bs_chain_tdo_i;   // External test
534:         `MBIST:             tdo_mux_out = mbist_tdo_i;      // Mbist test
535:         default:            tdo_mux_out = bypassed_tdo;     // BYPASS instruction
536:       endcase

543: begin

66: `include "adbg_or1k_defines.v"
67: 
68: module adbg_or1k_status_reg  #( 
69:         parameter NB_CORES = 4
70:     ) (
71:     input                       tck_i,
72:     input                       trstn_i,
73:     input                       we_i,
74:     input                       cpu_clk_i,
75:     input                       cpu_rstn_i,
76:     input  logic         [15:0] data_i,
77:     input  logic [NB_CORES-1:0] bp_i,
78:     output logic [NB_CORES-1:0] ctrl_reg_o,
79:     output logic [NB_CORES-1:0] cpu_stall_o
80: );
81: 
82:    reg   [NB_CORES-1:0] stall_bp, stall_bp_csff, stall_bp_tck;
83:    reg   [NB_CORES-1:0] stall_reg, stall_reg_csff, stall_reg_cpu;
84: 
85:    // Breakpoint is latched and synchronized. Stall is set and latched.
86:    // This is done in the CPU clock domain, because the JTAG clock (TCK) is
87:    // irregular.  By only allowing bp_i to set (but not reset) the stall_bp
88:    // signal, we insure that the CPU will remain in the stalled state until
89:    // the debug host can read the state.
90:     always @ (posedge cpu_clk_i or negedge cpu_rstn_i)
91:     begin
92:         if(~cpu_rstn_i)
93:             stall_bp <= '0;
94:         else
95:         begin
96:             for (int i=0;i<NB_CORES;i++)
97:             begin
98:                 if(bp_i[i])
99:                     stall_bp[i] <= 1'b1;
100:                 else if(stall_reg_cpu[i])
101:                     stall_bp[i] <= 1'b0;
102:             end
103:         end
104:     end
105: 
106: 
107:    // Synchronizing
108:    always @ (posedge tck_i or negedge trstn_i)
109:      begin
110:     if (~trstn_i)
111:       begin
112:          stall_bp_csff <= '0;
113:          stall_bp_tck  <= '0;
114:       end
115:     else
116:       begin
117:          stall_bp_csff <= stall_bp;
118:          stall_bp_tck  <= stall_bp_csff;
119:       end
120:      end
121: 
122: 
123:    always @ (posedge cpu_clk_i or negedge cpu_rstn_i)
124:      begin
125:     if (~cpu_rstn_i)
126:       begin
127:          stall_reg_csff <= '0;
128:          stall_reg_cpu  <= '0;
129:       end
130:     else
131:       begin
132:          stall_reg_csff <= stall_reg;
133:          stall_reg_cpu  <= stall_reg_csff;
134:       end
135:      end
136: 
137:    // bp_i forces a stall immediately on a breakpoint
138:    // stall_bp holds the stall until the debug host acts
139:    // stall_reg_cpu allows the debug host to control a stall.
140:    assign cpu_stall_o = bp_i | stall_bp | stall_reg_cpu;
141:

67: 
68: module adbg_or1k_status_reg  #( 
69:         parameter NB_CORES = 4
70:     ) (
71:     input                       tck_i,
72:     input                       trstn_i,
73:     input                       we_i,
74:     input                       cpu_clk_i,
75:     input                       cpu_rstn_i,
76:     input  logic         [15:0] data_i,
77:     input  logic [NB_CORES-1:0] bp_i,
78:     output logic [NB_CORES-1:0] ctrl_reg_o,
79:     output logic [NB_CORES-1:0] cpu_stall_o
80: );
81: 
82:    reg   [NB_CORES-1:0] stall_bp, stall_bp_csff, stall_bp_tck;
83:    reg   [NB_CORES-1:0] stall_reg, stall_reg_csff, stall_reg_cpu;
84: 
85:    // Breakpoint is latched and synchronized. Stall is set and latched.
86:    // This is done in the CPU clock domain, because the JTAG clock (TCK) is
87:    // irregular.  By only allowing bp_i to set (but not reset) the stall_bp
88:    // signal, we insure that the CPU will remain in the stalled state until
89:    // the debug host can read the state.
90:     always @ (posedge cpu_clk_i or negedge cpu_rstn_i)
91:     begin
92:         if(~cpu_rstn_i)
93:             stall_bp <= '0;
94:         else
95:         begin
96:             for (int i=0;i<NB_CORES;i++)
97:             begin
98:                 if(bp_i[i])
99:                     stall_bp[i] <= 1'b1;
100:                 else if(stall_reg_cpu[i])
101:                     stall_bp[i] <= 1'b0;
102:             end
103:         end
104:     end
105: 
106: 
107:    // Synchronizing
108:    always @ (posedge tck_i or negedge trstn_i)
109:      begin
110:     if (~trstn_i)
111:       begin
112:          stall_bp_csff <= '0;
113:          stall_bp_tck  <= '0;
114:       end
115:     else
116:       begin
117:          stall_bp_csff <= stall_bp;
118:          stall_bp_tck  <= stall_bp_csff;
119:       end
120:      end
121: 
122: 
123:    always @ (posedge cpu_clk_i or negedge cpu_rstn_i)
124:      begin
125:     if (~cpu_rstn_i)
126:       begin
127:          stall_reg_csff <= '0;
128:          stall_reg_cpu  <= '0;
129:       end
130:     else
131:       begin
132:          stall_reg_csff <= stall_reg;
133:          stall_reg_cpu  <= stall_reg_csff;
134:       end
135:      end
136: 
137:    // bp_i forces a stall immediately on a breakpoint
138:    // stall_bp holds the stall until the debug host acts
139:    // stall_reg_cpu allows the debug host to control a stall.
140:    assign cpu_stall_o = bp_i | stall_bp | stall_reg_cpu;
141: 
142: 
143:    // Writing data to the control registers (stall)
144:    // This can be set either by the debug host, or by
145:    // a CPU breakpoint.  It can only be cleared by the host.
146:     always @ (posedge tck_i or negedge trstn_i)
147:     begin
148:         if (~trstn_i)
149:             stall_reg <= '0;
150:         else
151:         begin
152:             for (int i=0;i<NB_CORES;i++)
153:             begin
154:                 if (stall_bp_tck[i])
155:                     stall_reg[i] <= 1'b1;
156:                 else if (we_i)
157:                     stall_reg[i] <= data_i[i];
158:             end
159:         end
160:     end
161: 
162:    // Value for read back
163:    assign ctrl_reg_o = {stall_reg};
164: 
165: 
166: endmodule

83:     input  logic                       module_select_i,

87:     // Interface to OR1200 debug unit
88:     input                              cpu_clk_i,    // 'bus' style interface to SPRs
89:     input                              cpu_rstn_i,
90:     output logic [NB_CORES-1:0] [15:0] cpu_addr_o,
91:     input  logic [NB_CORES-1:0] [31:0] cpu_data_i,
92:     output logic [NB_CORES-1:0] [31:0] cpu_data_o,
93:     input  logic [NB_CORES-1:0]        cpu_bp_i,
94:     output logic [NB_CORES-1:0]        cpu_stall_o,
95:     output logic [NB_CORES-1:0]        cpu_stb_o,
